THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Plaintiff submits to the Court's tentative ruling. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF DERRELL WHITLEY AN INDIVIDUAL Motion And Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel Initial Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two

PLAINTIFF'S motion to compel directed to the second set of document requests is GRANTED, with the direction that the parties meet and confer to ensure defendant's late-delivered document production is complete. Plaintiff is awarded sanctions in the amount of $1,500. Plaintiff's motion to compel directed to the second set of interrogatories is similarly GRANTED, with defendant ordered to provide amended responses in accord with Section 2030.220. Sanctions are denied. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLTE DETIALS)

Plaintiff's counsel is directed to prepare a proposed order consistent with the Court's tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and submit it to psw@hassard.com for review and signature.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Written Responses And Records To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Defense Counsel In The Sum Of $2,060.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities;
Off Calendar Feb-29-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DERRELL WHITLEY'S Motion To Compel Written Responses And Records To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Defense Counsel In The Sum Of $2,060.00

The unopposed motion is denied as it is untimely. The deadline for filing and service of the notice of motion to compel further responses was January 24, 2024 by the parties' agreement. While the service of the notice of motion was made on January 24, 2024, the motion was not filed until January 25, 2024. Therefore, it is untimely, and the Court has no choice but to deny this untimely motion. C.C.P. section 1005.5; Golf & Tennis Pro Shop, Inc. v. Superior Court, 84 Cal. App. 5th 127, 137 (2022). See also Weinstein v. Blumberg, 25 Cal. App 5th 316 (2018).

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
